Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- World model: Left green at cols 9-11 needs to move RIGHT into left frame (cols 12-14). Right green at cols 30-32 needs to reach right frame (cols 45-47) but purple bar (cols 33-38) blocks it. Clicking green center selects it for arrow key movement.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the pattern clearly from the history:
1. History 0 (initial): left=(18, W), right=(27, B)
2. History 1: MOUSE(31, 19) - clicked on left green center, no change (left still W)
3. History 2: MOUSE(31, 28) - clicked on right green center, centers swapped: left=(18, c), right=(27, W)
4. History 3: MOUSE(31, 19) - clicked on left green center, centers swapped back: left=(18, W), right=(27, c)
5. History 4: LEFT - left green moved from 18 to 15 (cols), center stayed W
6. History 5: LEFT - left green moved from 15 to 12 (cols), center stayed W
7. History 6: DOWN - left green stayed at 12, center stayed W (but later we saw it moved down in rows)
8. History 7: MOUSE(31, 28) - clicked on right green center, centers swapped: left=(12, c), right=(27, W)
9. History 8: MOUSE(30, 28) - clicked on right green area, no change
10. History 9: RIGHT - right green moved from 27 to 30 (cols), center stayed W
So the pattern is:
- MOUSE on right green center (row 31, col 28) swaps centers
- MOUSE on left green center (row 31, col 19) swaps centers
- LEFT moves the green with W center toward the left frame
- RIGHT moves the green with W center toward the right frame
But wait, in my last attempt, I clicked MOUSE(31, 31) which is the right green's current center position. But the right green is now at cols 30-32, so its center is at col 31. The click didn't work!
Looking at the history, the successful clicks were:
- MOUSE(31, 28) - when right green was at cols 27-29
- MOUSE(31, 19) - when left green was at cols 18-20
So the click position is FIXED, not relative to the green's position! The click positions are:
- Left green click: row 31, col 19 (fixed position, even when green moves)
- Right green click: row 31, col 28 (fixed position, even when green moves)
Wait, but that doesn't make sense either. Let me re-examine. In history 2, MOUSE(31, 28) swapped centers when the right green was at cols 27-29 (center at col 28). In history 7, MOUSE(31, 28) swapped centers when the right green was at cols 27-29... wait, no. In history 7, the right green was at cols 27-29? Let me check.
Looking at history 7's frame: left=(12, c), right=(27, W). So the right green is at cols 27-29. And MOUSE(31, 28) worked.
But in my last attempt, the right green is at cols 30-32 (center at col 31), and MOUSE(31, 31) didn't work. This suggests that the click position must be the ORIGINAL center position of the green, not the current one.
So the fixed click positions are:
- Left green original center: row 31, col 19
- Right green original center: row 31, col 28
Let me try MOUSE(31, 28) to swap centers, even though the right green is now at cols 30-32.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking at the ORIGINAL right green center position (31, 28)
# Current: left=(33-35, 15-17, c), right=(30-32, 30-32, W)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 28}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
